channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Polkadot - Mumbai Warrior Edition — 77 replies — 85.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - Mumbai Warrior Edition

Open thread • Replies: 77 • Updated: 2026-04-22 05:18 UTC

OP

Hello my fellow PolkaCurry brothers. We are again here, for another thread where we discuss everything related to DOT, Mumbai (the city of DOT), curry and more. The last thread was a resounding success, we managed to keep it alive for literally 41 days. We got paid 11500 + 5*150 = 12250 DOT for the thread and are now living in a period of enlightenment while eating with METAL spoons. Brothers, we have truly reached enlightenment. WE, the Mumbai Warriors will strike yet again and collect generations worth of money !

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

Early replies (first 3)

62115437
We are eating with our METAL spoons in Mumbai. No more hands my brethren. We eat with out spoons, like proud warriors.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62115624
>>62115424
>>62115437

Still gonna larp as an Indian?

62115793
>>62115437

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62121862
I'm not sure if you guys are looking at the chart but we're really close to breaking out from the bottom
Look at picrel >>62118020 and >>62118038

62121888
>>62121769
Will it have enough marketing?
No worries my brother. I have made a marketing contract with Jeny Smith (https://thothub.mx/videos/1505023/jeny-smith-polka-dot-pantyhose/). She will market DOT for us. We are set my Mumbai brother

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62121922
>>62121862
I'm not sure if you guys noticed. It's an adam and eve pattern on the 4h chart. Look at picrel

62121953
>>62121862
>>62121922

62122759
>>62121922
>>62121953

Chart looks good

But in the last thread weren't you shorting DOT

62123242
>>62122759
Panjali, be quite you fool (https://boards.4chan.org/biz/thread/61932834#p62069282). Do you want the Mumbai warriors to eat with their hands again? Or do you want to upgrade from our metal spoons to silver and then golden spoons? We, the Mumbai warriors, are painting the graph bullish in order to lure people in, then we strike with a x125 short. We actually been holding short open since 55$. The whole of Mumbai will eat from that singular short. Panjali, you have much to learn about the Vishnu ways. It's ok my son. I forgive you. Together we are strong, divided we are weak.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62124594
>>62123242
Way to promote a project then rug people

62125019
Didint read thread, i Will let My id decide if i buy some DOT when i get home

62125037
>>62125019
w4, E3, Wasb, and purple colour... Yes.. i see...

Not buying.

62125463
this is the most demoralizing dot thread

62126538
>>62124594
>>62125019
>>62125037
>>62125463
Good job my fellow warriors of Mumbai. Thank you for keeping the thread alive with your comments.

>this is the most demoralizing dot thread
How so my Mumbai brother?

>Way to promote a project then rug people
Yes Ganesha. We play the peoples hearts and then we stab them in their hearts.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62126677
Sol 8>297 Is 37X
Dot 1,15 x37= 42

62126931
>>62126538
Holy shit. Are you actually an autistic retard?

62126975
>>62126931
Kamal, are you getting upset again because your mother took away your metal spoon and ate with it? There is plenty of metal spoons for everyone my young warrior of Mumbai.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62127244
>>62126677
Vrishank, that is really good mathematics. Education is really paying off I see. Very good. You might even become a doctor or a lawyer. Yes, you shall reach to the heavens of nirvana one day my young Vrishank. I, Vishnu Kumar, believe in you and so should you my young Vishnu warrior of the mind chain, my young Mumbai warrior, my young metal spoon owner. Soon, we will have silver spoons for everyone, then gold spoon and after that we reach the nirvana of diamond spoons. The whole of Mumbai will eat.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62127578
>>62126975
Yep. You are officially a retard.

62127673
>>62127578
Vrishank, did you just really say that with the intent you did? Is there anything wrong? Are you not feeling ok?

62128205
>>62126931
>>62127578
you are talking to a bot (and so am I)

62128210
>>62128205
BIP BOP BIP BOP


Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62129040
>>62128205
No, its actual autistic retard

62129456
>>62129040
Impossible, look at the time stamps of the posts

62129518
>>62129040
>>62129456
No, it's actually Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62131415
100$ end of bull run


Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62131658
It is still cheap right now. Don't buy my bags at 25$. You can buy them now at 1.28$. Don't FOMO in at 50$ guys.

62131731
>>62116681
>What exactly does it have that sets it apart from Solona and ETH?
Solana has a success rate associated with it's transactions (see https://solscan.io/analytics). That's kind of fucked up that there's a 10 to 20% chance your tx might not get processed, right? I've never seen a DOT tx fail. DOT doesn't even measure failed tx https://data.parity.io/parachain-stats?selection-mode=include&frequency=daily&relay-chain=polkadot&chains= because there are none. If you submit a tx it'll complete within like 6s to 30s. The longest a tx takes is when you transfer from one corechain to another. So when you bridge (use a HRMP channel) between corechains. And even that never took longer than like 40s from my personal experience. It's bizarre to me that literally up to 20% of the tx simply fail.
Another thing is that SOL literally has more "vote" transactions than anything else; https://solscan.io/analytics#txs . And vote transactions are LITERALLY validator messages to keep the network running. DOT doesn't count that as a tx at all. 60 to 80% of the tx on SOL are from validators to keep the network running. That's insane to think about that you need more tx to run the network than the users use to execute stuff. People are stupid for thinking that SOL processes 3k TPS. More than half of that is just to keep the network running. It's a literal scam number to be honest. It's just to make the normies believe that the network isn't a scam. The number of transactions is completely fake in a sense.
And again, the funny thing is that 1 core of DOT will be able to handle more TPS than the whole of SOL once either JAM or NOMT hit. Maybe even before that once the 500ms Basti blocks hit. If you combine 500ms blocks + JAM + NOMT one core of DOT will be able to probably do anywhere between 2 to 5x more than SOL as a whole and DOT has LITERALLY 100 cores and this will also probably be scaled to more sooner or later. It'll sooner or later scale to about 300 to 500 cores.

62131777
>>62116681
>>62131731
The 300-500 cores are after the network upgrades to 1000 validators from 600 and after a few more future optimizations. But DOT can then scale further by adding more relay chains. So, currently there is 1 relay chain that connects all the corechains (while they also have direct connections through the HRMP channels) and this relay chain has 600 active validators at any given time. At some point DOT could scale further by having multiple relay chains and separate validators on each relay chain and then having a "relay chain" that connects the relay chains. Sure, this would fragment liquidity in a sense but you could still transfer assets between the relay chains in probably like 30s or so... So in a sense the fragmented liquidity would be a non-problem. DOT is truly scalable while other chains simply are not. DOT covered all the problems because Gavin used to develop ETH and noticed "OMG, this won't work out" and then he left ETH in order to develop DOT. DOT is ETH done right in a sense. ETH is just BTC with smart contracts and DOT is an actual solution to all the problems that plague ETH and all the other smart contract platforms.

62135297
100$ end of bull run

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62137662
Soon we shall reach the nirvana of 75$ my Mumbai brothers. Then we shall eat with silver spoons.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62139771
You are not the body, you are not the mind, you are not your emotions. You are the silent DOT in the infinity of consciousness. You are just a wave in the ocean of consciousness that forgot it is part of the ocean. For when rememberance dawns, the mystery is not solved. It is lived.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62141372
My brethren, the time is nigh. We're break out. The wave of consciousness is breaking free from the ocean floor.

item.title: Is Bill Ackmans closed ended fund PSUS going to make me rich? — 11 replies — 44.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Is Bill Ackmans closed ended fund PSUS going to make me rich?

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-04-22 06:05 UTC

OP

Is Bill Ackmans closed ended fund PSUS going to make me rich? I put in for 5,500 on shares. He's also throwing in free shares for PS which comes out the same day.

Early replies (first 3)

62136591
>>62136554
I think this has potential for making me a billionare

62136597
>>62136554
do you think Bill Ackman will outperform SPY or QQQ? if yes buy the shares. if not... dont.

62136620
>>62136597
I think he will out perform the S&P500 and I'm too afraid of tech stocks. Their valuation is high and a crash will kill me

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62136651
ACK-man

62136660
i wouldn't trust him, he wears too much makeup

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WEDIj9JBTC8

62136669
He’s looking to raise $5-10 billion. I find it hard to believe it can do anything more than an 8x over 10 years.

62136754
>>62136669
I want a lambo anon. I want a blue or red one

62137008
>>62136554
To the moon?

62137206
He's dumping on you and you're giving him money for it.

62141141
>>62137206
He wouldn't do that

62141446
smells like an ad desu

62138455

new low in April folks

62138857

62139101
what the hell. What if he ends up being right?

62141433

item.title: APU Coin is dead=? — 6 replies — 24.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
APU Coin is dead=?

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-04-22 06:08 UTC

OP

Biz family gathered around the plane that had been flagged this "APU APUSTAJA" and managed to completely destroy the plane.
remember when frog prices were floating around in early 2024? Everything was working, locals were playing football, and market capitalization rose to almost $200 million on July 24 after a massive sell-out, and Udinese football continued to play like the strongest team,

>the founder released Shadow Carpet on March 24 as a tribute to the fans and added a few ads but couldn’t resist
>the price of Bitcoin dropped to $83,000 in January 2026, and there was no real change until the end became clear in Rest Spot

No new relationships no major deals no $5 million on his best day, and the inscription on the X says he still has a loyal family but is silenced by fear and doubt like Pepe ass meater It seems to relate to another meme, the coin steals the show for, if you leave too soon you might lose your money,

but the current holder might lift the bag,
the bag is heavy and looks like a dead meme inside of a moon
What do you think about the senpai/biz/did apu or what?
family

Early replies (first 3)

62141318
I got tired of talking about any coins I like in here because it immediately gets flooded by a bunch of indians trying to FUD with the most retarded arguments (they are low IQ) or then the thread gets deleted by some stupid janny. APU is alive, just not in this board anymore, which is dead af. Fuck indians and fuck jannies.

62141333
>>62140906
Dead shitcoin.
>>62141318
Cope n seethe Kikelexander, or 5G, Ramza or whatever fag you are, not even SPX or any of the other coins from the Murad list dropped as badly as Apu did, that signals an incompetent team and a paperhand community.
It's dead, Apu is dead everywhere and nobody trusts your shitty "High Council" lmao.

62141373
Jannies and mods deleted every single thread after some time and ban you for talking about it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62141376
it looks like the poo's pumpy wumpy is finished
straight to zero
with all these link marines in your ranks it's no wonder you've become the cuckold of memecoins

62141385
They did the same shit to the Bobo, Hunny and the Bobina devs fuck them. I like Apu. Simple as. Faggots in a bucket, all of you.

62141452
>>62141385
>the Bobo, Hunny and the Bobina devs
All scammers

item.content:encoded:
>sold all my BTC at 76k thinking the war will continue and it'll dump what can I do now?

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-04-22 06:08 UTC

OP

>sold all my BTC at 76k thinking the war will continue and it'll dump
what can I do now?

Early replies (first 3)

62141402
Same, brother. I'm starting to think the bear market is over. The reality is that there is so much money and nobody wants to deal with the real world, so institutions and even people just buy assets.

What else are you gonna do? Real state is gay and you have to deal with the real world.

Just sit down at home and buy assets with your phone.

62141404
>>62141396
The fuck is happening? Dont twll me that the orange ape tacod, its boring af for the 12th time since the war.... Wtf is happening fo reel?

62141405
>>62141404
Bear market is over. There is so much money in the system, nobody wants to be left behind, cycles getting front run.... just up from here.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62141442
>>62141396
>the war

what fucking war? The russia china shit?

62141450
You did good, anon.

item.title: Do you flex your BTC to your family or nah? — 8 replies — 16.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Do you flex your BTC to your family or nah?

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-04-22 05:43 UTC

OP

Wondering if people actually talk about their BTC with family or just keep it lowkey.

>We never really know how long we have in life, so is it better to share it or keep it private?

Early replies (first 3)

62138671
>>62138453
I only have 1 so that's nothing to flex

62138680
People with more money than you won't give a shit and people with less money than you will resent you.

Keep your mouth shut and if anyone questions your purchases or your lifestyle, pretend to be in debt.

62138700
>>62138453
Keep that shit private, openly talking about how much money you have casually is trashy. Only discuss money with family with the intention of planning / sharing, otherwise they'll always have the thought that you have more to yourself. Speaking from experience with a good family, money brings out the absolute worst in people.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62139442
>>62138671
Okay, but what if something bad happens to you? You still won’t be able to pass it on even if it’s 1 BTC, even though its value could be much higher in the future.

62139556
>>62139442
>You still won’t be able to pass it on even if it’s 1 BTC,
What are you talking about kek. You sound confused

62139618
>>62138453
i make mom clean my room for 5 satoshis

62140241
>>62138680
Okay, I understand what you're saying about.

62141418
>>62138700
You also have a point. Do you keep it for your soon to be senpai or when you're married?

item.title: Censored by blue hair Revolut customer service agents for putting “kek” on my debit card. — 10 replies — 8.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Censored by blue hair Revolut customer service agents for putting “kek” on my debit card.

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-04-22 05:00 UTC

OP

Censored by blue hair Revolut customer service agents for putting “kek” on my debit card.

The point of this post is to document and highlight the level (((businesses))) will go to censor and bully their precious consumer.

>Today I decided to purchase Revolut’s ($150 annually) “metal” savings plan.
>5.50% apr. ok. whatever.
>The plan includes a customizable debit card.
>Subsequently, I wanted to get a little quirky and add the word "kek" to my *luxurious* metal card.
>My first design (pictured) was rejected due to supposedly being a depiction of drugs, alcohol, tobacco, firearms, weapons, or military equipment. Okay, fine.
>I then put #rekt which was also rejected for the same reason.
>I’m noticing a pattern here.
>I then decided to contact customer support, and to no avail, they wasted an hour and a half bullshitting over this matter, continuously creating excuses as to why they can't approve it nor escalate the issue to someone who can.
>At this point I truly think Revolut HQ is seeing war flashbacks every time they see the word “kek.”
>So anyways, I decided to do some studying about the founder of Revolut and Larry Fink whilst waiting.
>And of course, to no avail, nothing happens and I have to take matters into my own hands.
>After being rejected about 10 more times for attempting to submit my customizations through different variations of the word “kek”, and after all of this heart ache, blood, sweat, and tears, I finally got approved for my new card, which I’ve posted in the replies.

Early replies (first 3)

62138522
>>62138517
you are a faggot

62138603
OP will never have a gf because he is an autistic loser

62138610
>>62138517
shadilgay, my fellow redpillers!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62138842
>>62138610
Does it ever get boring being a misanthrope

62139509
>>62138842
You read all that and think OP gets bored? Only stupid people get bored .

62139518
>>62138517
give 0/10 customer service scores if you get prompted to make sure they have an unwanted convo with their supervisors.

62139519
>>62138517
This is based as hell. I also have an insatiable urge to fight injustices like this. It's a lonely life when you just don't bend over and take your spankings, but damn if it isn't fulfilling.

62139574
>>62138517
what's the goal? making cashiers physically cringe?

62139623
>>62138517
revolut limits crypto investments thats all you need to know you stupid nigger why even waste ur time on this ?

62141353
>>62139518
this, wagies need to do their fucking job. Not pretend like their position grants them any real authority.

item.title: INFLATION + RECESSION incoming - what do you buy? /biz/ — 6 replies — 5.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
INFLATION + RECESSION incoming - what do you buy? /biz/

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-04-22 05:08 UTC

OP

>global growth forecasts getting cut
>inflation not going away
>central banks stuck between a rock and a hard place
>stagflation looking likely
money is going to lose value AND economy slows down. classic worst case scenario.
what's the move?
GOLD?
SILVER?
BITCOIN?
S&P500?
WVCE?
CHAINLINK - ???
OTHER - what am i missing?
serious thread. what would you actually buy rn and why
long term investor here, not looking to flip. want something that's NOT overheated right now. everything feels toppy or already pumped. not looking for moonshots. just something with actual value that hasn't been bid up to retardation yet

Early replies (first 3)

62140772
>you

62140799
>>62140764
real estate for an easy 10x

62140803
you're drinking too much sperg doomer bullshit. The world is fine. Put most into diversified ETFs.

If you want to get rich, also invest in one or two single things you actually are putting in the work on. Listening to spergs and wanking on LINK is not putting in work.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62140974
If you are truly scared and don’t trust stocks at all, the move is pms. Gold specifically has beat the stock market the twenty years or so. Personally I’m 65% stocks, 20% bonds, and 15% pms, but I’ll be transitioning to a heavier pm load the more uneasy I become. Keep in mind that, historically, precious metals are not a great hedge against typical inflation; rather, it is a hedge against fiat in irrecoverable decline. If you don’t think that’s happening, pms might seem less attractive. Also, remember this: doomers always think things will happen faster than they actually will. The sky isn’t falling today and it probably won’t tomorrow either

62141039
>>62140764
I'd play income and gold balance with oil and commodities producers.

62141360
The greatest wealth one can achieve is the eternal life provided ONLY through accepting Jesus Christ as your Lord and Savior. It is a contract whereby entering into a covenant with Christ He has extended His New Covenant with God to you.

Put the fries in the bag, wagie

62128026
i already told you i don't have a job
if this weren't a retard thread you'd have come up with something funnier but it's blatant vapid spam
this site is fucked

62128031
avoiding flamewarring with me to keep the post count lower & thereby keep the thread up for longer
like i said this site is fucked, everyone is way too self-aware and there are shills around every corner and the mod team treats it like a top-down rat maze with no engagement

62128032

62128038

62128047

62128050

makes you wonder what the mods are doing, or at least it should
>>62123557

62128085

kek

62128168
>>62128038

62128292
>>62119165
>be me
>restaurant wagie in singapoor
>some mutt tourist think its funny to throw ketchup on the ground
>find mop and tell him to clean up
>hahaha no you are slave you do it
>call police
>he starts going off about first amendment and rights
>police gets agitatef because he doesnt do as they tell him, he argues like typical american
>they grab him to take into police car
>he starts fighting back, yelling about rights
>they taze him and beat him with police sticks
>take him to jail
I was kekking the entire time

62128310
>>62127025
Target and Trader Joe’s are can’t miss.

62130589
>>62125676
How did she manage to smuggle all that onto the flight?

62130590
>>62128292
Cope wagie. You still had to clean it up.

62130710

62130818

62132756

62132767

He drew comics.
Well, you either work in an office or youre some low iq blue collar.

trickle down (onto the floor) economics.

62133126
>>62130710

>>62132756
weak niggas still crying about the man lmao

62133190
>>62119165
lmao americans are actually so cucked
if a customer does stupid shit we throw him out and tell him to stfu haha

62133420
>>62130710
rip

62133812
>>62128292

He was just trying to enjoy a succulent Chinese meal

62133862
>>62128038
This is an accurate depiction of the person in the image. Some people are so embarrassingly pathetic then act like they're some edgy Nihilist. You've only got this one lifetime to repent your wickedness before you meet God

62133918
Why is being in the service industry so bad in America? Like something about it is just so demeaning and people are so shitty and rude.

Being a waiter in some small village in europe would prob be chill and relaxed.

62134155
>>62119443
this, except i have complete control over my life and do it out of malice/mischief

62134162
>>62130589
You are allowed to bring any non-liquid foods onto planes.

62134229
>>62125676
What a nightmare

62137688
>>62121327
I hear the calling of all my addictions at once, fuck this is beyond demoralizing.

62139836
>>62133862
g*d must repent first

62141222

62141226

item.title: Insider info here and i'm only sharing it because i'm a greasy nigger who's made millions from biz over the decade so wh… — 30 replies — 19.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Insider info here and i'm only sharing it because i'm a greasy nigger who's made millions from biz over the decade so wh…

Open thread • Replies: 30 • Updated: 2026-04-22 04:38 UTC

OP

Insider info here and i'm only sharing it because i'm a greasy nigger who's made millions from biz over the decade so whatever
there is a CRINGE coin you ALL know coming out with some state of the art shit very soon (already in the final stages of the audit)
Not giving the name because I'm not a total fag but trust me lol you all fucking know it lmao
Just remember you were too retarded to buy it at these levels and 5k could of put you in the top 50 holders lol
that'll be 150-180k within the next couple weeks lol some of you niggers might remember or know my wallet even
if someone guesses it i'll confirm

Early replies (first 3)

62141013

OP

Will our GRTies ever come back from obscurity? Or is it truly lost?

62128193
>>62128177

62128195
>>62128193

62128200
>>62128195

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62128208
>>62128202

62128224
>>62128208

62128247

62128257
>>62128247

I'm just thinking it's about time to jump back in

It's probably gonna start rallying up against Bitcoin now

62128260
>>62128177

62128262
>>62128260
Nah, GRT is built different

62128269
>>62128262
Yes and another 2021 rally is totally coming back again for digital nothing pixels. What makes your ponzi any different from the next ? Kek Baggies.

62128282
>>62128269

In about a year GRT will reach 2021 highs and I will be laffin'

62128644
GRT is going to $9.99. GRT, AMP and JASMY are Kek's chosen coins.

62128731
>>62128247
Too red eye, bless up rassclat. Yuns nuh wut da papa be singing! Ya no call dem ‘gaters long mouths till ya pass dem. We booyaka Babylon in due tim mon. Big up ya self natty dreads, when chicken marry, hawk dey near. But I be a broken record. They ai dat web3 wooooooo, faze me. Jah guide.

62129571
>>62128731

62129610

Silver linings and all that.

62129655

62129743
>>62129655

62130358

62130457

62131285

62133372

62133566

62133917

62133964

62134608

62136329

62136450

item.title: Something is coming soon — 29 replies — 9.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Something is coming soon

Open thread • Replies: 29 • Updated: 2026-04-22 03:04 UTC

OP

If you’re wondering where the recent FUD is coming from it’s because we’re only months away from the release of the CyberCode 2.0 testnet. As an insider, you have no idea what the importance of CbC 2.0 is going to be for LINK when the alpha releases around the end of November (give or take a few months) so I will spoonfeed you out of kindness of heart:

CyberCode 2.0 secures the ledger cryptographically to encode transactions at 20x of the legacy infrastructure. It achieves this by taking blockchain transactions to the BbC-2026 layer and parsing them through the node network incentivized by super linear sequencing (SLSeq) which Sergey said is very close to the finish line. This is all dynamically incentivized through semi-permeable staking (SPS) allowing LINK token holders to benefit for verifying data both on the main chain and the Blockchain Byzantine Consensus 2026 layer. No other project is even close to doing this in the oracle space rn. It’s not even close!

The obvious benefits of super fast transaction speeds (obSFTS) also brings exponential economies of scale. What this means is if farmer has 50 Kg pig for sale, he can put the beast to market on the blockchain to verify the weight in a trust less system faster than global SWIFT system. Soon any business who wants to stay relevant will have to buy LINK to have access to the CyberCode 2.0 feature. A truly unique proposition! Those who don’t use this system (powered by LINK) will get left behind. So get your ducks in a row. Just keep buying and NEVER sell. We are all in this together LINKbloodbros.

Scammer motherfuckers want you to think that banks and other organizations are not currently fulling their bags with cheap LINK as we speak. DON’T NOT LET THEM WIN! If you hold 50 or more LINK right now, you already made it. Immediately when we breach the $10 resistance it’ll be too late for you. You have been warned. So go buy many LINK for you dream to come true.

Early replies (first 3)

62125665
>>62125647
DR;NS

62125781
HOLY SHIT

By sheer chance I happened to be watching Sunny today, "The gang finds a dumpster baby", and recognised one of the frames Asslarper used (pic related).

In this episode, Dennis (AB's avatar) convinces an activist to CHAINLINK himself to a tree, then LITERALLY cuckolds him.

>A SINGULAR TREE
>THE cuckOLDS OF CRYPTO!

Now do you believe me?

Inverse Assblaster; what did he tell you was useless???

62125919
FUDDIES, ROLL OUT

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62125940
>BBC 2026

62125959
>MFW Stinkies still think they're relevant.

62125996
>>62125647
what if someone hacks the cybercode?

better wait a few more years for the PoC testnet beta to pass its initial audit round

62126130
This is pasta, right? I remember reading something just like this a long time ago

62126215
Holy fuck a good opportunity to buy a house with gf has come up and I might seriously have to sell my measly stack before it fuckin moons, I can't believe this shit Ive been holding for fucking years and years

62126221
>see the word "insider" on a /biz/ post
>hide thread

62126836
>>62125647
Delete this

62126992
>testnet

62127837
>>62125647
>technobabble word salad

62127858
>>62125647
LINK was supposed to moon with CCIP (See See I Pee) and steaking (full version will never be released btw). This CbC2.0 bullshit sounds like that BBC(A) shit that old jewish bitch at CLL came up with. Unfortunately the kind of jews at CLL are not the ones that make you money, but rather the scamming and subverting kind. We linkies will never makeit unless Sirgay unironically stops shoving bigmacs in every hole of his constantly (not happening).

62127911
the best part of this pasta is the farmer and pig

62130613
>>62125647
>BbC-2026
Hahaha very funny

62130614
Here's the real "something that is coming soon". Payment abstraction.

62130617
New things are coming with privacy technology, AI oracles also.

62131806
>>62130614
hasnt payment abstraction been a thing for like 3 years now, stinkie?

62131808
>>62125647

>two more copes

62131814

62131940
>>62125647

62131995
>>62131806
payment abstraction v2 right now, fuddie

62132082
>>62125647
>Sergey said is very close to the finish line
fucking lol, ok

62132691
>>62131940
if they made a sergey funko pop /biz/ would buy it

62133023
>>62125647

62134196
>>62126130

62135292
>>62127858

62137683
>>62127837

62141151

Were so back marines!

item.title: Just pull yourself up by your bootstraps — 7 replies — 8.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Just pull yourself up by your bootstraps

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-04-22 05:24 UTC

OP

Back then:
>meet wife in college
>graduate at 22
>get married
>make enough money at 26 to hire a famous custom home builder to make you a villa in the woods near NYC
>live comfortably for the next 74 years

https://youtu.be/q5OMBYKNXW8?t=375

Today:
>childhood mindfucked by the internet
>graduate college a virgin at 22 post GFC
>tens of thousands in debt
>unemployed
>unemployed
>parents threaten to kick you out unless you work or go back to school
>graduate college a second time at 26
>still unemployed
>finally get a job
>try to be social, can't because never met any developmental milestones
>things are looking up, saving tons of cash to put a down payment on a house
>COVID 19
>years of life gone by in a flash
>everyone is mindfucked by the internet
>people retreat into hiding, avoid making eye contact, no one smiles
>young women actively hate you
>old infertile hags start to show interest and get pissed when you don't chase them
>still living with parents at 36
>burnt out, no energy to do anything outside of work except doomscroll
>down payment money turns into planning for retirement fund, just another 35 years to go

Early replies (first 3)

62141243
loser lol

62141249
>>62141240
the job market needs to improve this is just insane man

62141256
>>62141240
>Siting Boomers
Don't hate the players when the game is rigged and the rules change the later you play.
>What used to work for them hasn't worked or been relevant for decades.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62141262
>>62141256
He's 100 years old, he predates even the boomers that bought suburban cookie cutter houses. He lived in a time where you could just do whatever the fuck you wanted like building a hippie commune in the woods without the government razing it to the ground and murdering your wife and son.

62141329
>>62141240
We have some things in common but totally different outcome
>childhood mindfucked by the internet
>graduate high school and quit RuneScape cold turkey
>go to college and make friends, party, and do drugs
>graduate college a virgin at 22 post GFC
>"only" thousands in debt because of HOPE scholarship and grants
>accepted to post-graduate program
>work while in school and graduate at age 26
>$150k+ in student loan debt
>immediately promoted from intern to job paying $110k
>move out of parents place and get cheap apartment
>try to get gf but too focused on making money
>discover crypto in early 2017 and eventually make enough to pay off student loans
>COVID 19
>happy to stop going out with almost all friends because of lockdown
>annual income approaching $200k
>investments really paying off and interest rates in the toilet so I buy a house and new Tesla
>NW reaches $1 million
>get practice gf
>dump practice gf
>NW reaches $2 million
>get new gf 10+ years younger than me
>quit job and move to Thailand at age 36

62141336
>>62141256
I'm going to sue my mom so she literally dies in prison because she committed fraud against me. I will 'hate the player' and fags like you can sob and cry on behalf of someone else's terrible parents.

62141381
>>62141329
Based, way to save the thread. I'm sick of complainers. They're not wrong but what a way to spend your life.

item.title: How much BTC you holding right now? — 102 replies — 7.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
How much BTC you holding right now?

Open thread • Replies: 102 • Updated: 2026-04-21 16:33 UTC

OP

How much BTC do you currently have in your wallet, and what’s your target amount to hold before it reaches $1M?

>I mean, if you don’t even have at least 1 BTC and you’re in crypto, it still feels pointless.

Early replies (first 3)

62114988
>>62114930
1.03

ill keep buying as pay cheques come in as long as we stay around the 4 year SMA

ill stop buying when we start running up and instead start selling to buy other assets, probably pay off my mortgage

62115186
>>62114930
i cope with my 5 stack and tell myself in 10 years i'll make it.

62115188
>>62114930
350 BTC or so.

Feels good. I'm living pretty modestly in a $2MM 5000 square foot house in a low cost state. Probably going to build a home gym extension with a sauna and hyperbaric oxygen chamber. Also a small fallout shelter just to be comfy.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62126533
>>62114930
https://timesofmalta.com/article/elusive-bitcoin-founder-adam-back-lives-malta.1126648
What are the financial implications of this?

62126561
>>62123814
What risk are you talking about here? Also, it’s better now that there are returns instead of just holding with no yield. This setup is way better overall.

62126610
>>62117395
but why did you sell?

62126617
>>62117832
buy the bottom? but at which price?

62127919
>>62124487
More!!!

62128353
>>62120218
YOU CAN DO IT BOI

62128654
>>62125560
Don't worry about it. Keep going and have fun. YOLO.

62130077
>>62122141
Why FUD

62130099
0.3

62131443
>>62130099
Just 0.3? Make it whole bitcoin

62131447
>>62126507
since the COVID boom? It was just $8k during covid

62131531
>>62114930
The new paradigm is no more peaks.

62131569
>>62114930
Zero and zero

62132043
1.72 bitcoins
*sigh*
I keep stacking, maybe one day, I'll have 2 whole bitcoins.

62132222
around 10, i might end up keeping them forever as a backup plan. Looking at my taxes my average accumulation price was 29k and sell price was 109k Tidy little 70k each. I'll buy back in when the dump is over and it settles between 20-30k. for supposedly being a crypto board you all sure seem to struggle with basic pattern recognition, this is exactly what happens every time, dump 75% from the peak and then the next bull run starts.

62132243
>>62115773
I remember that 21 used to be the suistack. Everyone's goal was to accumulate AT LEAST 21 btc.

62132565
>>62131531
Why?

62133350
>>62131447
61k in March 2021.

62133953
>>62131569
That's bad.

62136283
>>62133350
I saw the chart

62136286
>>62132043
You can always make it. Start small

62136448
>>62116911
>I’m actually selling all my crypto this week. Cope and seethe
Doing a lot of cope and seething huh?

62136454
>>62115225
>Macbook
You might as well shared your dildo collection anon.

62137580
>>62136454
>not using the most cost-effective general purpose laptop in 2026
Yeah, you're ngmi

62138430
>>62136448
Why did you do that ?

62138432
>>62136454
lmao

62138468
Had several BTC and loads of ETH back in college when I sorted fake ids for my friends, lost it all on a physical flash drive wallet. BTC was only worth ~$20k a coin so I'm not at the point of offing myself over losses yet lmao

62138538
>>62138468
That was your fault tho.

62138592
>>62138538
Yup, got panicked and boxed up all that bs when someone reported me to the RA. Thought I eventually packed it with me when I moved, probably got nicked by someone. Good times.

62138882
I basically missed every pump and now I hold onto 0.35 BTC and I’m never selling it, ever. I sometimes get really mad that I had 15 at one point, could’ve easily had more but I let every so-called “export” and fudder convince me it was worthless. I could’ve been retired at the age of 21 and now I gotta wage it out until I’m 65 assuming AI doesn’t kill us.

item.title: Structure and Interpretation of Financial Markets — 32 replies — 4.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Structure and Interpretation of Financial Markets

Open thread • Replies: 32 • Updated: 2026-04-21 22:38 UTC

OP

I'm using this thread as a place where we can thoughtdump stuff on interpreting market structure for a better read on what's going on. I am not good at this so could use some help or any input. A lot of fintwit retards are saying the recently SPY rally was 'obvious', and I didn't see it coming in so fast and sharp, so I'm looking into some means of better understanding underlying market structure to make better predictions. Some examples:

>inference of dealer positioning (GEX, etc) and understanding of the dynamics that follow from their positioning (eg vanna, charm, etc)
>institutional flows (JHEQX, JPM collar, CTAs, corporate buybacks, etc)
>expiry dates (OPEX, vixperation, etc)
>sec filings
>hedging flows, put call parity, etc

Early replies (first 3)

62136340
>>62136320
markets dont like unexpected events so they sell off when they occur. over time markets learn about the situation and often start to recover. see covid, tariffs, and iran as recent examples.

62136346
market crabbing = house taking all bets in waiting for imbalance to occur
market not crabbing = imbalance occurring
simple as
>vanna, charm
not simple as
now why that imbalance (or no imbalance) is occurring and who the players are causing said balance/imbalance is a different conversation... ... ... (niggers & jews)

62136398
>>62136340
yes. but this is due to exogenous events, what I'm looking to better interpret is endogenous market structure.

>>62136346
How are you observing said imbalance? Are you reading L2 order book data? What are you intending to imply by your comment on vanna and charm?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62136430
>>62136398
>What are you intending to imply by your comment on vanna and charm?
i dont make trading decisions based on any greeks other than delta. especially not the second order derivatives.
>How are you observing said imbalance?
i just "feel it in my bones" ya know?

62136441
>>62136430
>i dont make trading decisions based on any greeks other than delta. especially not the second order derivatives.
Why do you not see any value in gamma / gex for example to infer dealer positioning at given price levels?

>i just "feel it in my bones" ya know?
no, I dont' know

62136544
>>62136320
No hate, but are you the same guy from this

>>62130967

thread?

62136555
Random walk with positive drift

62136556
>>62136544
indeed I am

>>62136555
yeah but the walk is influenced by positioning and flow of capital, which is what I'm trying to better codify here

62136570
>>62136346
jesse what the fuck are you talking about
>>62136320
just pay attention to what the FED and institutional banks are doing, that's usually all you need to do

62136588
>>62136398
>i am not good at this so could use some help or any input. A lot of fintwit retards are saying the recently SPY rally was 'obvious'
>markets dont like unexpected events so they sell off when they occur. over time markets learn about the situation and often start to recover. see covid, tariffs, and iran as recent examples.
>what I'm looking to better interpret is endogenous market structure.
ok buddy lol. dont say people didnt try to help you.

62136592
>>62136570
I'm trying to improve my ability to read mechanical market state, like reading what the market is telling me. The FED and banks are exogenous factors that the market reacts to and should be monitored closely of course but are outside the endogenous mechanics of the market.

The FED might make a decision that causes a reposition of the major players in the market, and if you can read the market well you can infer what it expects now, for example, and that can indicate volatility and direction.

62136601
>>62136556
My assumptions on you based on what you wrote:

>pretty high IQ
>good at what he does, decent trader
>turbo autism
>virtually zero ability to understand what motivates people
>likes numbers/data
>tunnel vision, overfocusing
>no idea what is going on in the world

How close am I?

Personally I would love to hear what your guess is on why this war is happening.

62136607
>>62136592
i see
well good luck

62136625
>>62136588
you're not helping though. everyone knows the market derisks under increasing uncertainty and violently sells off during 'black swans'. this is common knowledge and doesn't relate to what's being asked in the OP.

You're trying to point out that once we had more information regarding the outcome of the situation in the Middle East, the market rebounded due to the increasing certainty that came from taking wild scenarios (WW3, etc) off the table. That was expected. What was not expected was the slingshot to $710, and this largely a mechanical move due to the market being overhedged (short squeeze) and a lot of gamma around $685 due to the JPM collar. That's my opinion after the fact anyway, I was not plugged in enough during the rally to see the mechanics unwind and that was a mistake I'm trying to fix.

62136638
>>62136601
>virtually zero ability to understand what motivates people
>tunnel vision, overfocusing
What do you mean here? Are there things I'm not factoring in that I should be?

>How close am I?
I do a lot better trading purely quantitatively / algorithmically. That's usually what I do, but it's slow to build out infra so I don't have all the strucutural/mechanical/microstructure stuff I'd like yet and the market lately has been dominated by geopolitics and Truth social post that aren't as friendly to that kind of stuff. The people that *did* have the ability to read the mechanical state of the market during the rally were seemingly in a pretty good spot to catch a once in a lifetime move, so the opportunity cost of not being setup to catch those moves or at least hedge against explosive upside was huge during this, so I'm prioritizing it a lot more. I think a lot of it is probably very nuanced too and it'd take a fuckton of historic data to backtest everything required (as in, beyond just market data but including JPM collars, put call parity, institutional inflows, etc - it'd be a pretty extreme task and could take years to get to the point), so I'm trying to learn to do subjective, interpretive market reads to be a better discretionary trader or influence my automated system using some discretionary influence.

If you could've read the market well, you could've bought a far OTM call going into the 4/17 OPEX, during the first week of April and it would've absolutely printed. I don't really know what's going on in the war aside from the strait being closed throughout and I doubt Israel is going to let us off the hook so easily, Trump seems to want a graceful exit but idk if Iran or Israel will give him that. But he's typically ok with just lying and forgetting about stuff like that. Mechanically OPEX reset so we're looking at fresh dealer positioning and I'm betting on a move down to ~$695-700.

62136648
Learn ICT trading concepts. ICT is the SICP of trading.

62136657
>>62136648
lol good one retard

62136729
>>62136638
>What do you mean here? Are there things I'm not factoring in that I should be?
I very much like the idea of automation so I'm rooting for you but your desire to separate the data/numbers from the external world is going to miss things in times like these. You're not incapable of incorporating outside factors into an algorithm, you might want to make a system that helps you track what companies are pro or anti this or that, but sometimes people intentionally lie for ulterior motives.

Maybe just design the system so it is able to identify pattern-breaking moments like these. Then label it an "anomaly" even though in reality the concept of anomaly basically just means "something I don't understand."

On most days I don't believe in free will so I believe the majority of people not only don't understand what's happening, even if you told them they would just reject it and it would actually be impossible for them to accept it.

62136816
>>62136729
>You're not incapable of incorporating outside factors into an algorithm
It's a long term goal of mine, it just introduces complexity. I don't even know what backtesting that would really even look like either or how you'd design a learner over that. What I can do though is incorporate the underlying mechanical forces that result from these real world events, and feed that to the learner, so I'm trying to focus that down. I think that's more fundemental and generalizable too. Where I think solid edge would lie for real world event interpretation would be more in live dialogue during FED meetings or earnings, since you could get in before the market fully restructures. I just don't know how you'd design a learner that could navigate something novel like the Iran war unfolding combined with a president actively contradicting things. It seems like it'd be easier to just design something that follows the trend soon after he posts and hope it's correct most of the time.


My automated system actually performed decently enough through this whole thing, but it mostly does intraday only, so it wasn't really capable of capturing that longer term trend which would've been extremely profitable. My discretionary trading was bad though and that's what I need to iron out first. This rally minted many new millionaires and I wasn't one of them, but I feel if I had both more skill in interpreting the mechanical factors driving the market as well as the infra to display it, I could've done significantly better. Allotting even $2k to hedge insane upside early April on SPY $700 4/17 calls would've been an insanely profitable and reasonable play if you were aware of the squeeze potential and gamma positioning around $685/700 and the heightened volatility that goes into the monthly OPEX, imo. It's something that crossed my mind repeatedly, I just wasn't organized enough mentally or workflow wise so I'm spending this week building out some stuff to help there.

62138084
>>62136320
>I am not good at this
You don't need to be.
The market is fake and gay. You can make some money with it but it won't be life-changing money.
Work, live below your means, build a rainy day fund, accumulate BTC and play with shitcoins for beer money. That's all you have to do. Trading is a money sink.

62138221
>>62138084
No, this is my only chance and you can make life changing money with it. The most recent rally was a perfect example. There's also strategies that exist that will get you there, whether you can fish them out is another question.

62138356
>>62138221
It's a fool's errand. The market is manipulated and it reacts to your movements. You lose by design, now even more with AI and HFT. The 1% of traders that squeeze the money out of the other 99% (that would be you) have insider knowledge.
Best you can do is invest mid or long term. Set it, forget it and work in the meantime. You think this is your last chance because you have been brainwashed, and you're right on track to give them all your money, rather sooner than later. I know that you're not gonna listen and keep on thinking that you can outsmart them. You can't. Because it's not about being smart, it's about being solvent. And you're a miriad times less solvent than the ones you're about to give your money to.

62138393
>>62138356
I think you're underestimating the alpha a low capacity retail trader can extract and frankly being a bit of a doomer. If you run an efficient, statistically sound strategy (or otherwise provide services to the market) and keep up with the non-stationarity of markets (ie adapt), you can make money.

It's not about 'outsmarting' them or challenging their solvency, either. It's about finding statistical edge, wherever that may come from. In this thread for example, I'm not looking to outsmart anyone - I'm doing the opposite. I'm trying to read the room and see what the big players are doing and how they're positioned to better trade with their flows. I am in fact abandoning the idea that the market will correct towards my personal bias and instead intending to solve for their bias and trade with it.

62138401
>>62138393
Go ahead, do it. I'll see you in a few years when you get wiped out on a single candle

62138414
>>62138401
I've been doing it. I don't run any real risk of getting wiped out, you're conflating trading with lack of risk management. Bigger risk would be death by a thousand cuts over several months/years but I'd like to think I'd have the sense to shut things down or adapt before then.

62138928
>>62136320
So where my head is currently at is that there is no magic ETF flow (obviously) or anything like that, but where I suspect this investigation would lead is in some combination of using forecasted numbers from big banks and the FED as a baseline for expectation / disruption and intent (ie, JPM and BofA both have positive annual forecast and imply buying pressure throughout the year), use that as a base case that things revert toward unless sufficiently disrupted. This would be easy enough to backtest too, probably (if you could historical forecast from big banks/funds). You could see what kind of deviation from the expectation causes a breakdown etc.

Then on top of that, to catch something like the April 1st pivot, where you wouldn't know if this whole 'Iran war' (substitute for whatever current event) is a forecast changing event, assume the base case from above holds and look for mechanical fingerprinting in the order flow streams that CTAs, vol-targetters, risk parity traders and options dealers would show. I think that's where everything really comes together - look at what they look at, model it how they model it (the best you can), try and use the same signals, etc. The point wouldn't be to trade those signals, but use them to infer their behavior from the higher ups.

If CTAs drove the rally, there was probably a lot of mechanical execution taking place across sectors and assets they monitor. Basically I think if you could model and identify that fingerprint you could say something like
>this looks like mechanical buying flow from CTAs, this implies we're risk on again
And you could react a lot quicker and if you had a better idea of the options structure / dealer positioning, you could watch price get shoved into places that cause even more buying or short covering, which is what we saw imo.

62138945
>>62138928
This is all really hand wavy and without the ability to distinguish between 'price went up' (random walking) and 'this is CTAs buying' (fingerprinting) it's useless. IDK if it's feasible to do that but it's something to check out anyway.

And you'd have various regimes and interactions too like between different volatility levels, risk parities, dealer positioning, etc that you'd have to figure out.

62139238
>>62136320
>some means of better understanding underlying market structure

Machine Elves. Our brains like to make patterns out of noise. These hallucinations impact how people behave and it creates a cyclical relationship in the market. So what patterns have been drilled into human brains over millennia?

62139242
>>62139238
>what patterns have been drilled into human brains over millennia?
boobs and butts?
